channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Italy's far-right Prime Minister's political future at stake in referendum vote
item.description: Italian Prime Minister's close relationship with President Trump may weigh on a referendum on reforming Italy's judiciary.
item.content:encoded: Italian Prime Minister's close relationship with President Trump may weigh on a referendum on reforming Italy's judiciary.
item.dc:creator: Megan Williams

item.title: NATO allies prepare for a Russian attack in the far North
item.description: Despite tensions, U.S. and other NATO nations conduct exercises in the far North off Norway to prepare for Russian attack.
item.content:encoded: Despite tensions, U.S. and other NATO nations conduct exercises in the far North off Norway to prepare for Russian attack.
item.dc:creator: Teri Schultz

item.title: Israel's war strategy involves high-level assassinations. But will it win the war?
item.description: The war between the US, Israel and Iran is intensifying. NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Yossi Melman, who reports on Israeli intelligence, about the state of the conflict as it enters its fourth week.
item.content:encoded: The war between the US, Israel and Iran is intensifying. NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Yossi Melman, who reports on Israeli intelligence, about the state of the conflict as it enters its fourth week.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: What a team of investigative reporters found out about Banksy's identity
item.description: A Reuters investigative team discovered intriguing details about Banksy's identity after seeing his art pop up in Ukraine.
item.content:encoded: A Reuters investigative team discovered intriguing details about Banksy's identity after seeing his art pop up in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: The effects of the Iran war on environmental and human health, according to an expert
item.description: As the war in Iran enters its fourth week, the costs are adding up. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Doug Weir, with the Conflict and War Observatory, about impacts to human health and the environment.
item.content:encoded: As the war in Iran enters its fourth week, the costs are adding up. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Doug Weir, with the Conflict and War Observatory, about impacts to human health and the environment.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Trump threatens Iran's power plants as war enters fourth week with no end in sight
item.description: We have the latest on the U-S and Israeli war on Iran, where in the past 48 hours, Israel has struck one of Iran's nuclear facilities and Iran has responded with strikes in Israel.
item.content:encoded: We have the latest on the U-S and Israeli war on Iran, where in the past 48 hours, Israel has struck one of Iran's nuclear facilities and Iran has responded with strikes in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants as Iran strikes 2 Israeli cities
item.description: Iran launched missiles at two southern Israeli cities that lie close to the country's main nuclear research center, while President Trump gave Iran 48 hours to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
Iran launched missiles at two southern Israeli cities that lie close to the country's main nuclear research center, while President Trump gave Iran 48 hours to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Ohad Zwigenberg)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Cortina d'Ampezzo mixes Olympic legacy with Alpine glamour
item.description: Cortina d'Ampezzo, the "Pearl of the Dolomites," is a blend of Olympic heritage with celebrity chic, fine dining and Alpine tradition, even as climate change and new tourism reshape the area.
item.content:encoded:
Cortina d'Ampezzo, the "Pearl of the Dolomites," is a blend of Olympic heritage with celebrity chic, fine dining and Alpine tradition, even as climate change and new tourism reshape the area.

(Image credit: Valerio Muscella for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Cuba begins to restore power after third nationwide collapse in a month
item.description: Cuba's power grid collapsed Saturday leaving the country without electricity for a third time in March as the communist government battles with a decaying infrastructure and a U.S.-imposed oil blockade.
item.content:encoded:
Cuba's power grid collapsed Saturday leaving the country without electricity for a third time in March as the communist government battles with a decaying infrastructure and a U.S.-imposed oil blockade.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A strike on a hospital in Sudan killed at least 64 people, WHO says
item.description: At least 64 people were killed, including at least 13 children, in a strike on a hospital in Sudan's western Darfur region last week, the World Health Organization said Saturday.
item.content:encoded: At least 64 people were killed, including at least 13 children, in a strike on a hospital in Sudan's western Darfur region last week, the World Health Organization said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
